channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: SCOOP: Pence urges Republicans to hold the line on tax hikes for the rich as Trump weighs options
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence told House Republicans to "stand firm" and make the "Trump-Pence" tax cuts permanent as the White House floats a millionaire tax hike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: Federal officials are circulating a draft budget proposal that would make dramatic additional cuts to federal health programs and serve as a roadmap for more mass firings.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas measles cases are underreported, response hurt by funding cuts, CDC scientist says
item.description: A CDC senior scientist provided an update on the measles outbreak on Tuesday, while taking reporter questions about the agency's response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump hints at firing Federal Reserve chair Powell after tariff comments
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s termination “cannot come fast enough” as he reiterated his frustration that the Fed hasn’t aggressively cut interest rates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Clashing Over Campus Control: Lawmakers vs. DeSantis on transparency
item.description: For years, lawmakers have speculated that the leaders who run the state's Universities and Colleges have been gifted these positions based on their political loyalties to the Governor.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Boasberg V. Trump
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Underperforming DC schools' staff has grown 7 times faster than enrollment: audit
item.description: An audit of D.C. Public Schools' found staffing levels in the district have increased at a rate of seven-and-a-half times more than student enrollment, underscoring a larger trend nationwide of public school districts serving fewer and fewer students while staffing grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state senator flies to El Salvador to visit deported migrant in prison, only to be denied
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen, D-Md., flew to El Salvador on Wednesday to visit Kilmar Abrego Garcia, but the country's vice president denied him access to the maximum security prison on short notice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EPA can't end grants from $20 billion Biden-era fund for climate-friendly projects, a judge says
item.description: A federal judge says some nonprofits awarded billions for a so-called green bank to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects cannot have their contracts scrapped and must have access to some of the frozen money.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance to meet Modi, Meloni during trip to India and Italy with wife Usha
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance and his family will travel to Italy and India this week and next to meet with leaders and visit cultural sites.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge: 'Probable cause' to hold U.S. in contempt over Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The government sent several planeloads of alleged gang members to El Salvador, including 137 people under the act, the White House said at the time. The judge gave the U.S. until April 23 to respond.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Retail sales jump as people rush to buy cars ahead of tariffs
item.description: The key driver of the economy saw the biggest increase in over two years in March, as car buyers tried to get ahead of President Donald Trump's tariffs on imported autos and auto parts.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: NIL payments OK at UNLV, UNR, but payment amounts secret under SB293
item.description: A bill concerning NIL — name, image, likeness — and how UNLV and UNR can manage contracts directly with players passed on Tuesday in the Nevada Senate, advancing to the Assembly with other bills. A Republican leader objected to secrecy surrounding the payment amounts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: Trump did not attend any of the annual dinners during his first term, bucking a tradition that went back several administrations.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Bipartisan senators rebuke White House move to end legal aid for unaccompanied minors
item.description: The letter obtained by NPR marks a rare bipartisan critique from Capitol Hill of the administration's immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Getting the IRS on the phone is more difficult this tax filing season, experts say
item.description: Taxpayers calling the IRS for help processing their taxes this filing season may find it harder than normal to get someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House doctor says Trump is 'fully fit' in medical report
item.description: President Trump lost 20 pounds, lowered “bad” cholesterol and has “diverticulosis and a benign polyp,” according to a medical report released by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: A retrial of Sarah Palin's case against 'New York Times' begins Monday
item.description: Former Alaska Gov. Sarah Palin has the rare opportunity to retry her defamation case against The New York Times even though she lost it — twice in a 24-hour-period — in early 2022.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence. It began gaining momentum after the Supreme Court’s 2022 ruling overturning Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Sisolak rematch with Lombardo? Former governor says he's considering it
item.description: Former Gov. Steve Sisolak is considering challenging Gov. Joe Lombardo in 2026, he told Politics Now host John Langeler on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC
